source_paper_title: Lower Complexity Bounds of First-Order Methods for Affinely Constrained Composite Nonconvex Problems
context:
Section 4 (Conclusion and Open Questions), page 25.

First, for Algorithm Class 1 on solving Problem Class 1, can the lower bound \(O(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) be achieved, i.e., is it tight?
title: Tightness of the \,\(\Omega(\kappa([\bar A;A])\,L_f\,\Delta F_0\,\varepsilon^{-2})\) oracle lower bound for Algorithm Class 1
background_latex:
Let \(d,n,\bar n\in\mathbb{N}\). Consider the affinely constrained composite optimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d} F_0(x):= f_0(x)+g(x)\quad\text{s.t. }Ax+b=0,
\]
where \(A\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times d}\), \(b\in\mathbb{R}^n\), \(f_0:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) is \(L_f\)-smooth (possibly nonconvex), and \(g:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) is proper, lower semicontinuous, convex (possibly nonsmooth). Assume further the structural condition (Problem Class 1) that \(g(x)=\bar g(\bar A x+\bar b)\) for some \(\bar A\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n\times d}\), \(\bar b\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\) and proper lower semicontinuous convex \(\bar g\) with \(\mathrm{relint}(\mathrm{dom}(\bar g))\neq\emptyset\) and \(\mathrm{dom}(\bar g)\) not a singleton.

Define the stacked matrix \([\bar A;A]\in\mathbb{R}^{(\bar n+n)\times d}\) and its condition number
\[
\kappa([\bar A;A]) := \sqrt{\frac{\lambda_{\max}([\bar A;A][\bar A;A]^\top)}{\lambda^+_{\min}([\bar A;A][\bar A;A]^\top)}}.
\]
Let \(x^{(0)}\) be the algorithm initialization and \(\Delta F_0 := F_0(x^{(0)})-\inf_{x} F_0(x)\).

An \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point (Definition 1 in the paper) is a point \(x^*\) for which there exists \(\gamma\in\mathbb{R}^n\) such that
\[
\max\Big\{\mathrm{dist}\big(0,\nabla f_0(x^*)+A^\top\gamma+\partial g(x^*)\big),\ \|Ax^*+b\|\Big\}\le \varepsilon.
\]

Algorithm Class 1 consists of first-order methods that, at each iteration, may query the oracle
\(\mathrm{ORACLE1}(x,z,\eta)=(\nabla f_0(x),Ax,A^\top z,\mathrm{prox}_{\eta g}(x))\), and whose iterates are generated by linear-span rules specified in the paper (Algorithm Class 1). The paper proves a worst-case lower bound that any such method may require \(\Omega(\kappa([\bar A;A])\,L_f\,\Delta F_0\,\varepsilon^{-2})\) oracle calls to reach a near \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point.
problem_definition_latex:
Problem: Determine whether there exists a first-order method in Algorithm Class 1 whose number of calls to \(\mathrm{ORACLE1}\) needed to compute a (near) \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point of every instance in Problem Class 1 is bounded above by
\[
O\big(\kappa([\bar A;A])\,L_f\,\Delta F_0\,\varepsilon^{-2}\cdot \mathrm{polylog}(\varepsilon^{-1})\big),
\]
i.e., prove the lower bound \(\Omega(\kappa([\bar A;A])\,L_f\,\Delta F_0\,\varepsilon^{-2})\) for Algorithm Class 1 is tight up to at most logarithmic factors, or else prove that any Algorithm Class 1 method must have a strictly larger worst-case oracle complexity order.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
